data.title: Stocks drop, hurt by DoJ probe of UnitedHealth, downbeat economic data
data.description:
U.S. stocks fell Friday on word of a government investigation into UnitedHealth (UNH) and gloomy economic updates, driving Wall Street's major averages toward losses for the week.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was -0.5%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) -0.6%, and the Dow (DJI) -0.8%, after Thursday's loss of 1%. Eight of the 11 sectors on the S&P 500 (SP500) fell, led by Consumer Discretionary (XLY) -1.2%.

UnitedHealth (UNH) led decliners on the Dow (DJI). Shares sank -9% after The Wall Street Journal reported the U.S. Department of Justice has launched an investigation into the company’s Medicare billing practices. UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) declined to comment, and the company later criticized the Journal over its reporting.

Meanwhile, investors were handed some dour economic reports. The February S&P Composite PMI fell to 51.6 from the 52.7 prior print. The service index dropped to 49.7 from 53.0. “U.S. business activity growth came close to stalling in February as a renewed fall in services output offset faster manufacturing growth,” S&P Global Market Intelligence said.

“The upbeat mood seen among U.S. businesses at the start of the year has evaporated, replaced with a darkening darkening picture of heightened uncertainty,” Chris Williamson, chief business economist at S&P Global Market Intelligence, said in the report.

Also, the University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index slid to 64.7 in February's final reading, missing the 68.0 consensus, clipped by worries that tariff-induced price increases are approaching.

Separately, January U.S. existing home sales slumped -4.9% to 4.08M versus the 4.13M projection.

The Dow (DJI) for the holiday-shortened week was looking at a fall of ~1.6%. The S&P 500 (SP500) this week notched two record closing highs, but was also facing a weekly decline.

In the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was fell 5 basis points to 4.46%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was lower by 3 basis points to 4.24%.

U.S. stocks fell Thursday, in part as investors balked at Walmart's (WMT) soft financial outlook.

data.title: Technology may make asset prices more efficient, but algorithms may err, Fed vice chair says
data.description:
Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip N. Jefferson on Friday stressed the importance of central bank communications in transmitting monetary policy and the use of technology in assessing the effectiveness of that communication.

"The configuration of short-term interest rates, central bank communication about the likely future path of short-term interest rates, and the associated economic outlook, in turn, affect long-term interest rates through investors' expectations," Jefferson said in prepared remarks for a speech at a San Francisco Fed research conference.

"By conveying aspects of the Fed's reaction function, communications can help inform investors' views about the likely future path of monetary policy in a way that helps achieve the Fed's monetary policy objectives," he explained.

Transparency and technological advances have potentially made asset prices more "informationally efficient, which, in turn, helps with the transmission of monetary policy," he added. But it's not quite that simple. "Others argue that automated algorithms may be more prone to mistakes than humans, may provide an incentive for investors to value speed over accuracy, and may reduce the long-run informativeness of asset prices, which could hurt the transmission of monetary policy."

He also addressed the use of artificial intelligence. So far, it hasn't changed how policymakers communicate, "but research shows that it has affected how quickly information about policy is incorporated into asset prices," he said.

Jefferson also noted that research is showing that consumers appear to react more to information from social media, friends, and family than from traditional media. "All told, this research suggests that central bank efforts to communicate with the general public are having some success, but there is still room for improvement," he said.

He concluded his prepared remarks, noting that "a wealth of data and techniques to analyze text does not necessarily translate into greater insight. Therefore, it is important that policymakers, researchers, and investors continue to be diligent in using the right tools and the right data to make the best possible inferences."

Breaking news... check back for updates.

data.title: Farmer Mac targets $300M farm securitizations in 2025 amid renewable energy growth
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Federal Agricultural Mortgage Corporation (NYSE:AGM) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Brad Nordholm highlighted record net effective spread and core earnings in 2024, driven by consistent loan growth and effective asset-liability management. Two $300 million farm securitization transactions were completed, marking the first time the company executed two such issuances in one year.

  • The quarterly common stock dividend will increase by $0.10 to $1.50 per share starting Q1 2025, representing a 7% increase from 2024. Nordholm described this move as a balance between earnings growth and maintaining adequate capital levels.

  • CFO Aparna Ramesh noted that 2024 core earnings amounted to $171.6 million or $15.64 per diluted share. She emphasized the proactive management of the balance sheet through debt funding strategies and hedging capabilities.

  • The company rebranded its Rural Utilities segment to Power & Utilities and introduced a new Broadband Infrastructure segment. Nordholm described the latter as critical to rural economic development, with $300 million growth in 2024, or 60% year-over-year.

Outlook

  • The company plans to continue $300 million targeted deal sizes for farm securitizations in 2025, supported by ongoing exploration of new securitization products and asset classes, including renewable energy.

  • The Renewable Energy segment pipeline remains robust with plans to double its portfolio volume again, as it has annually since 2020. Management sees significant growth opportunities in energy storage and renewable energy generation.

  • Nordholm assured that no material changes are anticipated due to the change in administration in Washington, D.C., while the company continues to monitor regulatory developments.

Financial Results

  • Core earnings for Q4 2024 were $43.6 million or $3.97 per share. Total revenues improved to $362 million in 2024 from $349 million in 2023, primarily driven by a shift towards higher-spread businesses.

  • Net effective spread increased by $12.6 million year-over-year but compressed by three basis points to 115 basis points due to non-accrual loans and a more volatile funding environment. Sequential improvement in Q4 2024 spread to $87.5 million was driven by proactive equity capital allocation and renewable energy portfolio growth.

  • Operating expenses rose 18% sequentially in Q4 2024, primarily due to technology costs, licensing fees, and legal expenses related to growth in broadband infrastructure and renewable energy segments.

  • Allowance for losses increased to $25.3 million as of December 31, 2024, reflecting borrower-specific headwinds and new volume in infrastructure finance. Substandard asset volume rose to $440.7 million.

Q&A

  • Bill Ryan, Seaport Research Partners, asked about a potential transformational securitization product. CEO Nordholm responded that feasibility work continues on securitizing Farm & Ranch and renewable energy loans, noting no pending announcements but emphasizing ongoing exploration.

  • Bose George, KBW, inquired about net effective spread trends for 2025. Nordholm and CFO Ramesh explained that proactive funding strategies and a shift to higher-spread businesses should maintain spreads within a range of 112-116 basis points, depending on rate movements.

  • George also asked about the impact of renewable energy growth on credit quality. Nordholm noted that credit risks remain idiosyncratic and manageable, with no systemic issues identified.

  • Unidentified Analyst questioned the adequacy of loan loss reserves. SVP Marc Crady clarified that increases in reserves were not catch-ups but reflected idiosyncratic borrower-specific issues and volume growth in higher-risk segments.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed interest in the company’s growth in renewable energy and securitization but raised concerns over rising operating expenses and credit reserves. The tone was neutral to slightly positive.

  • Management's tone was confident, particularly in discussing strategic initiatives and financial performance. However, a defensive tone emerged when addressing credit quality and expense increases.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, analyst sentiment was similar, while management appeared slightly more optimistic due to sustained revenue growth and strong pipelines.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, with no major changes in strategic focus. However, the introduction of new segment reporting and rebranding efforts indicate a shift towards greater transparency and focus on broadband and renewable energy growth.

  • Analysts in both quarters focused on renewable energy and securitization. Management’s confidence in these initiatives has increased, supported by strong pipelines and market demand.

  • Credit reserves increased, and operating expenses rose sequentially, reflecting investments in growth segments.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified borrower-specific credit issues, noting higher substandard assets and increased allowances for losses.

  • Analysts raised concerns over volatility in AgVantage volumes and the potential impact of regulatory changes on renewable energy tax credits.

  • Management emphasized their ability to adapt to market and regulatory changes, supported by strong capital and liquidity positions.

Final Takeaway

Farmer Mac delivered record financial performance in 2024, driven by strategic growth in renewable energy, broadband infrastructure, and securitizations. The company’s plans for $300 million targeted farm securitizations and robust pipelines in renewable energy signal continued growth in 2025. While credit quality and rising expenses pose challenges, management remains confident in its ability to navigate market cycles and regulatory developments, demonstrating strong execution against long-term objectives.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Booz Allen, CACI, Leidos, Parsons all plunge as Blair cuts on DOGE risk
data.description:
Booz Allen (BAH) -3.7%, CACI International (CACI) -3.5%, Leidos (LDOS) -2.6%, Parsons (PSN) -4.3%, and General Dynamics (GD) -0.5% in Friday's trading as William Blair downgrades all five defense tech services companies to Market Perform from Outperform on the heightened risk of budget cuts.

Defense Scoop reported Thursday that all U.S. Department of Defense agencies were told to begin a thorough review of consulting services on a contract-by-contract basis and terminate "non-essential" consulting programs, which followed Secretary of Defense Hegseth's order to cut programs and reset spending to better align with President Trump’s priorities.

While it is unclear what constitutes non-essential consulting services, DOGE and the Treasury Department this week announced the termination of a $1.9B IRS IT modernization blanket purchase agreement contract, and Leidos (LDOS) enterprise IT contract for the Social Security Administration also was canceled.

While share prices of defense tech service companies have fallen off post-election highs in anticipation of spending disruptions, William Blair analyst Louie DiPalma believes "the consulting memo represents a deterioration, [and] that a number of contracts could be at risk for termination, and the pipeline of new contracts will decline."

DiPalma thinks consensus estimates generally assume a minimal impact from DOGE and new policies, as an example citing Booz Allen’s (BAH) FY 2026 consensus revenue growth of 8%, which is at the high end of prior long-term guidance for 5%-8% growth.

Blair reiterates Outperforms rating on AeroVironment (AVAV) and Curtiss-Wright (CW), a Market Perform recommendation on Science Applications International Corp. (SAIC), and an Underperform rating on Palantir Technologies (PLTR).

ETFs: (BATS:ITA), (NYSEARCA:PPA), (NYSEARCA:SHLD), (XAR)

data.title: Zoom Q4 earnings on deck: What to expect
data.description:
Zoom Communications (NASDAQ:ZM) is set to post results for the holiday quarter on Monday, after markets close.

Wall Street expects the California-based videoconferencing company to post Earnings Per Share (EPS) of $1.35, while revenue is expected to rise 3.5% to $1.19 billion during the quarter.

Zoom, which became a household name during the pandemic, experienced slow growth during the recent years, largely due to a return to workplaces and stiff competition from bigger rivals including Microsoft Teams. To tackle the decline, the company kept on expanding its offerings with a slew of new AI-powered products to boost demand.

The communications software maker recently changed its name to Zoom Communications from Zoom Video as it looks to focus on artificial intelligence.

Analysts are mostly positive on the stock, given its new product initiatives. Seeking Alpha analysts and Wall Street rated the stock a Buy.

Jefferies analyst Samad Samana believes that Zoom’s AI monetization could drive revenue growth, and the brokerage is expecting “solid” Q4 results due to continued Enterprise momentum.

“We also see room for mgmt to guide FY26 above consensus given low expectations, sustained ARPU expansion in Enterprise, and a continued Online stabilization,” said Samana.

Over the last two years, Zoom has beaten both EPS and revenue estimates 100% of the time.

“ZM's products and initiatives beyond video conference such as Virtual Agents, Workvivo, and Contact Centre have showed tremendous progress, potentially resolving its growth deteriorating,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant ratings are cautious and consider it a Hold, mostly dragged down by valuation and growth factor.

Over the last three months, EPS estimates have seen 23 upward revisions, compared to three downward revisions. Revenue estimates have been revised upwards 25 times versus two downward moves.

The company, which has a market value of over $25 billion, has risen nearly 2% so far this year.

data.title: Deregulation exposure was the most valuable characteristic of a stock owned by hedge funds – GS
data.description:
Goldman Sachs strategists saw notable thematic shifts within their portfolios due to post-election policies after President Donald Trump’s win.

Changes in the fourth quarter for hedge funds reflected many of the “consensus Trump trades,” said Ben Snider, senior strategist on the U.S. portfolio strategy macro team at Goldman Sachs.

He said that hedge fund ownership of potential deregulation beneficiaries, stocks with small business exposure, and domestic sales increased in the fourth quarter compared to the previous quarter.

Among these potential deregulation beneficiaries are Capital One Financial Corp. (COF) – what he calls “a new VIP,” – and the “rising stars” Stifel Financial Corp. (SF), Tesla (TSLA), and The Williams Companies (WMB).

On the other hand, hedge funds reduced their ownership of stocks with exposure to the China supply chains, international sales, and tariffs risk.

Here is an overview:

  • Stocks that would benefit from deregulation increased about 25 basis points within the share of hedge funds’ ownerships during the fourth quarter of 2024.
  • Financial deregulation and small business exposure beneficiaries saw about 15 basis points increases.
  • Domestic sales exposed stocks saw about 8 basis points increases.
  • China sales exposed stocks saw decreases, however a small change – about -1 basis points.
  • Stocks with China supply change and international sales exposure saw about -12 basis points change in hedge fund positioning.
  • Lastly, stocks exposed to the tariffs risk saw about -20 basis points change in hedge fund ownership.

Goldman analysts also listed stocks that have U.S. government exposure risk and their short interest as a percentage of float as well as their total return year-to-date.

  • Novavax, Inc. (NVAX) – Change in short interest since Nov. 5: -2 pp; Total return YTD: 2%
  • Emergent BioSolutions Inc. (EBS) – Change in short interest since Nov. 5: -0 pp; Total return YTD: 2%

data.title: EU regulators set to charge Google for breaking DMA laws - report
data.description:
EU competition watchdogs are ready to charge Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) for breaching the bloc's Digital Markets Act rules, Reuters reported Friday, citing people with direct knowledge of the matter, after its proposed changes to its search results failed to address their concerns and those of their rivals.

The tech giant has been under investigation since March last year for potential breaches of the DMA.

The EU's DMA laws serve as "gatekeepers" against big tech's influence, which ensures that their online presence is fair and leaves room for competition without dominating.

Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) has been under scrutiny for its search business practices for allegedly favoring its own offerings, such as Google Shopping, Google Flights, and Google Hotels, over rivals and discriminating against third-party services on Google search results. The imminent charges concerned this issue, sources told Reuters.

The other investigation takes aim at potential limitations that hinder app developers from informing users about offers outside the Google App Store free of charge, the report said.

In November, reports suggested that Apple (AAPL) was set to face the first-ever fine under the DMA for big tech. However, the iPhone maker has been making efforts to adhere to the DMA rules. Meta Platforms (META) is also under probe for potential violations under the DMA since March.

In the U.S., Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (GOOGL) was found guilty by the Justice Department for having a monopoly in its online search business. The DoJ has been forcing the company to sell its Chrome web browser, which the tech giant calls “extreme.”

data.title: Uniti Group targets $1.43 AFFO per share in 2025 with focus on fiber expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Uniti Group (NASDAQ:UNIT) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Kenny Gunderman highlighted that 2024 was the company's most consequential year, citing strategic moves that have "future-proofed" the business. He emphasized recurring revenue growth of approximately 5%, adjusted EBITDA growth of 8%, and consolidated bookings growth of 27%, underscoring strong demand for fiber infrastructure. He also announced that Uniti expects to generate positive free cash flow in 2025.

  • Gunderman discussed the company's merger with Windstream, which is expected to close in the second half of 2025, possibly as early as July. He noted that the merger would simplify their MLA relationship and position Uniti to benefit from the growing demand for Generative AI and fiber-to-the-home convergence.

  • CFO Paul Bullington reported Q4 consolidated revenue of $293 million, adjusted EBITDA of $239 million, and AFFO per diluted share of $0.35. He also noted that net success-based capital expenditures were $0.7 million, highlighting effective cost management.

Outlook

  • Uniti projects 2025 AFFO per diluted share to range between $1.40 and $1.47, with a midpoint of $1.43, representing a 6% increase from 2024.

  • For Uniti Leasing, the 2025 revenue and adjusted EBITDA are projected at $902 million and $872 million, respectively, with $185 million allocated for success-based CapEx.

  • Uniti Fiber expects 2025 revenue of $304 million and adjusted EBITDA of $125 million, with capital intensity expected to reach 28%.

  • The company anticipates building fiber to 2 million homes by the end of 2025, two years ahead of its original target.

Financial Results

  • Consolidated Q4 revenue was $293 million vs. analysts' estimate of $294.59 million. Adjusted EBITDA stood at $239 million, while AFFO per share was $0.35.

  • Uniti Leasing achieved revenues of $222 million and an adjusted EBITDA margin of 97%. Uniti Fiber contributed $72 million in revenues with a 43% adjusted EBITDA margin.

  • Combined gross capital expenditures were $24.3 million, offset by upfront customer payments of $23.6 million, resulting in a net success-based CapEx of $0.7 million.

  • The company's 2024 results aligned with its guidance, despite lower-than-expected AFFO per share due to incremental interest from its $300 million secured notes add-on.

Q&A

  • Greg Williams, TD Cowen: Asked about timing and opportunities in the inference phase of AI in Tier II and III markets. CEO Gunderman explained that inference spending is expected to grow to 80% of total AI-related spending by 2030, emphasizing Uniti's strategy to secure first-mover advantage in Tier II and III markets through its fiber network.

  • Frank Louthan, Raymond James: Inquired about ABS (asset-backed securities) funding mix and fiber build targets. CFO Bullington stated that Uniti would continue to grow its ABS funding while remaining flexible with other debt instruments based on market conditions. Gunderman added that Uniti plans to build 325,000 homes in 2025 and accelerate marketing efforts to increase penetration rates, which are currently 25%-30% for recent cohorts.

  • Bora Lee, RBC Capital Markets: Asked how hyperscaler needs have evolved. Gunderman noted a significant increase in strand counts and conduit capacity requirements, with the company now deploying cables with over 864 strands, compared to 6-12 strands a few years ago. He highlighted opportunities for additional fiber leases as hyperscalers return for more capacity.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed a mix of curiosity and optimism, focusing on AI opportunities, ABS funding strategy, and Uniti's merger progress. Questions reflected an interest in the company's ability to maintain competitive yields and execute its fiber expansion plans.

  • Management maintained a confident tone during the prepared remarks, frequently emphasizing strategic execution and growth opportunities. In the Q&A session, Gunderman addressed questions with detailed responses, expressing optimism about Uniti's positioning in core markets and the benefits of its Windstream merger.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone remained consistent, with a continued focus on execution and strategic growth. Analysts' tone showed slightly more optimism regarding wireless bookings and hyperscaler demand.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language evolved to include more specific AFFO and EBITDA targets for 2025, reflecting increased confidence in financial performance and merger synergies.

  • Strategic focus shifted towards accelerating the fiber build plan, with updated targets for 2025 and beyond. Wireless bookings transitioned from muted in Q3 to showing early signs of recovery in 2025.

  • Analysts' focus remained on hyperscaler demand and AI opportunities, while management reiterated its disciplined approach to fiber expansion and lease-up strategies.

  • The tone of both management and analysts showed a slight increase in optimism, particularly regarding the Windstream merger timeline and its potential benefits.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted challenges in maintaining competitive yields in hyperscaler deals and the potential complexity of integrating Windstream's operations.

  • Analysts raised concerns about ABS funding reliance and whether capital allocation would sufficiently balance fiber expansion and marketing efforts.

Final Takeaway

Uniti Group's Q4 2024 earnings call showcased strong recurring revenue growth, disciplined cost management, and strategic positioning for future expansion. The company remains focused on its merger with Windstream, accelerating its fiber build plan, and capturing opportunities in AI-related infrastructure. With 2025 AFFO per share targeted at $1.43 and plans to build fiber to 2 million homes by the end of the year, Uniti appears well-positioned to meet growing demand in Tier II and III markets.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Grab Holdings' FY25 outlook will likely prove conservative - analyst
data.description:
J.P. Morgan upgraded Grab Holdings (NASDAQ:GRAB) to Overweight from Neutral on the expectation for GRAB’s FY25 adjusted EBITDA guidance to prove conservative and headline earnings to drive positive revisions in EPS expectations. This is predicated on GRAB’s ability to historically beat EPS guidance set at the beginning of each year.

Better cost management should not only support earnings growth but result in better platform health and top-line growth through more affordable services, J.P. Morgan’s Ranjan Sharma says, adding that a large untapped market for GRAB in Southeast Asia remains, growing monthly transacting user (“MTU”) beyond the addressable market and adding to mid-term earnings growth.

Advertising is another growth driver, specifically for revenue and margins, as ad revenues have grown to 1.7% of deliveries gross merchandise value in Q4 vs 1.4% a year ago. In Q4, the number of active advertisers who jointed GRAB’s self-serve platform increased by 63% year-over-year and spent 21% more.

“Given GRAB discloses return on ad sales at 5-8x and merchant retention is high at ~75%, we anticipate advertising penetration to deepen and support delivery revenues and margin growth,” said Sharma.
